sections.paragraph:
θεατρίζω (ins fr. Gerasa: JRS 18, 1928, 144ff, no. 14, 18 [c. 102–14 a.d.], where the word certainly appears, though its mng. is uncertain; s. HCadbury, ZNW 29, 1930, 60–63; Achmes 21, 5 ἀτίμως θεατρισθήσεται; 51, 11; Suda II 688, 26; Byz. Chron. in Psaltes p. 328; ἐκθεατρίζω in Polyb. 3, 91, 10 and Diod S 34+35 Fgm. 2, 46) put to shame, expose publicly ὀνειδισμοῖς τε καὶ θλίψεσιν θεατριζόμενοι publicly exposed to reproach and affliction Hb 10:33 (Posidon.: 87 Fgm. 108 [app.] Jac. ἐξεθεάτριζον ὀνειδίζοντες).—DELG s.v. θέα. TW.

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

JRS JRS = Journal of Roman Studies—List 6

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Posidon Posidon , II–I b.c.—List 5

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 446
